entry.title: Intercompany STO - Use Sales Price (ESP2) as Purchase Price in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We are implementing an Intercompany Stock Transfer scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario

Delivering Plant: 1050 (Company Code 1010, Germany)
Receiving Store: 3940 (Company Code 3910, Austria)

Our requirement is to determine the purchase price of the intercompany stock transfer from the sales price condition (ESP2).

Currently, the standard pricing procedure uses condition type PPR0, which determines the price via Purchasing Info Records.

We created our own condition type ZVPV as a copy of PPR0. However, in the Partner Demo system the access sequence cannot be modified because it is restricted.

Our questions are:

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to determine the intercompany purchase price directly from the sales condition ESP2?
Is maintaining Purchasing Info Records the SAP standard approach, or is there a recommended alternative?
Is the restriction of changing Access Sequences only a limitation of the Partner Demo system, or does the same restriction exist in productive Public Cloud systems?
What is the SAP Best Practice for this scenario?

We have attached screenshots showing:

Purchase Order Pricing Analysis
Pricing Procedure
Sales Order Pricing (ESP2 determined successfully)

Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,
Mustafa Kocabeyoglu

entry.title: Re: sapui5 - updating the bootstrap to release 1.149 / 1.150
entry.content:
SAPUI5 1.149 and also 1.150 are cloud-only deliveries. So you won't get them for any ABAP onPremise system.

See also SAP Note 2217489.

The notification you got is only about SAPUI5 patches that are planned to be deleted on the SAPUI5 CDN, which is the cloud-channel for consuming SAPUI5. So, this should have actually not impact on your onPremise system - at least not for minor versions. But you must only check that the latest patch on your onPremise system is still available on CDN in case you are using any SAPUI5 application on premise with SAP Work Zone.

entry.title: Force password change for Dialog user IDs on next login due to security policy change
entry.content:
Due to a change in Security policy, we have to force all the dialog user IDs to change the password in the next login. Is there any option available to force password change for Dialog user IDs on next login without a system restart?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Force password change for Dialog user IDs on next log...
entry.content:
As the KBA 3460608 mentions:

"Existing passwords can be forced to change to the new rules on next login by setting the parameter login/password_compliance_to_current_policy = 1 (this still does not affect System and Service users, only Dialog and Communication)"

 

Please also read the KBA 3495035:

  • Refer to the documentation of the parameter login/password_compliance_to_current_policy, which can be accessed in transaction code RZ11.
  • This parameter controls whether the system checks if the password used for a password-based logon fulfills the current password rules and, if necessary, prompts the user to change their password.
  • If the parameter is set to 1, users of type DIALOG and COMMUNICATION, that do not fit the new rules defined by password/logon parameters, will be required to change their password on next password-based logon. 
  • If the parameter is set to 0, DIALOG and COMMUNICATION will only have to change the password once it expires, which is controlled by parameter login/password_expiration_time.     
  • For COMMUNICATION user, Although the system checks whether the password has expired or is initial, the implementation of the requirement to change the password, which exists in principle, depends on the logon method (interactive or non-interactive). 
  • Users of types 'SERVICE' and 'SYSTEM' are excluded in principle from the requirement to change passwords and are therefore not affected by this parameter.

 

 

The login/password_compliance_to_current_policy is a 'Dynamic Parameter', therefore does not require a system restart.

entry.title: Re: CJ88 getting error- Not possible to post down payment...
entry.content:
First, ensure that the sum of down payments and down payment clearings equals zero in all depreciation areas. If this condition is not met, error AA 571 will occur.

If down payments and their clearings are posted in a prior fiscal year (and are therefore not considered in the current year's settlement), it is necessary to refer to SAP Note 2725359 - Investment measure settlement: Settlement of sender costs from previous years.

Also the following KBA can be helpful too:

3470077 - Error AA571 issued during settlement to asset

entry.title: Fixing Sort, Group & Reset in Fiori Elements with CAP CDS What's Not Standard and What's Needed
entry.content:
The Problem

In a SAP CAP Fiori Elements List Report or Object Page application, you might find that Sort, Group, and Reset personalization features do not work out of the box, especially when your entity uses navigation properties (associations) as display columns.

Symptoms

  • Clicking Sort on a column throws a backend error or does nothing
  • Group dialog shows columns that cannot be grouped, causing OData errors
  • Reset or Restore button does not work, or personalization state is lost on page refresh
  • Search works but sort and group personalization is broken

Example Scenario

Consider an entity that displays details from an associated entity:

entity OrderItems {
  key order   : Association to Orders;
  key product : Association to Products;
}

Your @ui.LineItem annotation displays navigation properties:

annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(UI: {
  LineItem: [
    { Value: product_ID },          // flat key - works fine
    { Value: product.name },        // navigation property - breaks sort
    { Value: product.category },    // navigation property - breaks sort
    { Value: order.customerName }   // navigation property - breaks sort
  ]
});

Fiori Elements will show all these columns in the Sort and Group personalization dialog. But the OData backend cannot sort on navigation properties like product.name. Attempting to do so results in a runtime error.

What is Standard vs. What You Need to Add

Standard (works out of the box)

Feature Works For

SortingFlat or direct properties of the entity (e.g., product_ID, order_ID)
GroupingFlat or direct properties
Table rendering@ui.LineItem, @ui.PresentationVariant annotations
Search@CDS.search annotation on the entity

NOT Standard - You Must Configure These

The following three fixes are required when your table displays navigation properties.

Fix 1: SortRestrictions for Navigation Properties

When your @ui.LineItem displays columns from associations, Fiori Elements will offer these in the Sort and Group dialog by default. But the OData backend cannot sort on navigation properties.

The Fix

Add Capabilities.SortRestrictions.NonSortableProperties to exclude navigation properties from sort and group:

annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(
  Capabilities: {
    SortRestrictions: {
      NonSortableProperties: [
        product_ID,
        product.name,
        product.category,
        product.description,
        order.customerName,
        order.status
      ]
    }
  }
);

What This Does

  • Tells Fiori Elements not to offer these columns in the Sort or Group personalization dialog
  • Prevents runtime OData errors when users try to sort
  • The columns still display in the table. They just cannot be sorted or grouped.

When to Use

  • Any time your @ui.LineItem references a navigation property (e.g., product.name, order.type.description)
  • Deep navigation paths like order.statusRef.name

Fix 2: data-sap-ui-flexibility-services for Personalization Persistence

By default, Fiori Elements personalization (sort, group, filter variants) requires a flexibility service to store the user's settings. Without it, the Reset button may not work and personalization state is lost on refresh.

The Fix

Add the following attributes to your app's index.html bootstrap script:

<script
    id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
    src="https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-core.js"
    crossorigin="anonymous"
    data-sap-ui-theme="sap_horizon"
    data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
        "com.mycompany.myapp": "./"
    }'
    data-sap-ui-oninit="module:sap/ui/core/ComponentSupport"
    data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
    data-sap-ui-async="true"
    data-sap-ui-frameOptions="trusted"
    data-sap-ui-flexibility-services='[{"connector": "SessionStorageConnector"}]'
></script>

Key Attributes

Attribute Purpose

crossorigin="anonymous"Required for proper CORS handling of the UI5 library resources
data-sap-ui-flexibility-servicesConfigures where personalization state is stored
SessionStorageConnectorStores personalization in browser session storage (development or standalone)

Production vs. Development

  • Development or Standalone: Use SessionStorageConnector. It stores personalization in browser session storage.
  • Production on BTP Launchpad: The SAPUI5 Flexibility Service handles this automatically via the Launchpad shell. You typically do not need this attribute when deployed to a managed Launchpad.
  • Standalone production apps: Use SessionStorageConnector or configure a persistence backend.

Fix 3: @ui.HiddenFilter for Non-Filterable Fields

If your entity exposes fields via associations that should not appear in the filter bar's "Adapt Filters" dialog:

annotate MyService.Products with {
  description  @UI.HiddenFilter;
  category     @UI.HiddenFilter;
  manufacturer @UI.HiddenFilter;
  weight       @UI.HiddenFilter;
}

What This Does

  • Hides these fields from the "Adapt Filters" dialog in the List Report
  • Prevents users from trying to filter on fields that may not be efficiently queryable
  • The fields still display in tables. They are just not offered as filter options.

Complete Example

Here is a complete annotation setup for an entity with navigation properties:

// Entity definition with associations
entity OrderItems {
  key order   : Association to Orders;
  key product : Association to Products;
      quantity : Integer;
}

// Annotations
annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(
  // Prevent sort and group on navigation properties
  Capabilities: {
    SortRestrictions: {
      NonSortableProperties: [
        product_ID,
        product.name,
        product.category,
        product.description,
        order.customerName
      ]
    }
  },
  UI: {
    PresentationVariant: { Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem'] },
    LineItem: [
      { Value: product_ID,          ![@UI.Importance]: #High },
      { Value: product.name,        ![@UI.Importance]: #High },
      { Value: product.category,    ![@UI.Importance]: #Medium },
      { Value: order.customerName,  ![@UI.Importance]: #Medium },
      { Value: quantity }
    ]
  }
);

// Hide non-filterable fields from the Adapt Filters dialog
annotate MyService.Products with {
  description  @UI.HiddenFilter;
  category     @UI.HiddenFilter;
  manufacturer @UI.HiddenFilter;
}

Summary

Feature Standard? What to Add Where

Sort on flat propertiesYesNothingN/A
Sort on navigation propertiesNo, failsSortRestrictions.NonSortablePropertiesCDS annotations
Group on flat propertiesYesNothingN/A
Group on navigation propertiesNo, failsSortRestrictions.NonSortablePropertiesCDS annotations
Reset personalizationMay not workdata-sap-ui-flexibility-servicesindex.html
Personalization persistenceLost on refreshSessionStorageConnectorindex.html
Hide fields from filter dialogShows all@ui.HiddenFilterCDS annotations
CORS for UI5 resourcesMay warncrossorigin="anonymous"index.html

Key Takeaway

Fiori Elements is powerful but assumes your OData entity is flat. When you use CAP associations and display navigation properties in tables, you must explicitly tell the framework which columns are non-sortable and non-groupable. Without this, the personalization dialogs offer options that crash at runtime.

The three things to always check:

  1. SortRestrictions for every navigation property in your @ui.LineItem
  2. flexibility-services in your index.html for personalization to work
  3. @ui.HiddenFilter for fields that should not be in the filter dialog

References

  • CAP CDS Annotations: Capabilities
  • Fiori Elements: Configuring Tables
  • SAPUI5 Flexibility Services

entry.title: Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) deletion
entry.content:
Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) deletion  

With the release of SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS1, SAP delivers deletion report for Object-Based Authorization (OBA) framework for Project System. It helps administrators efficiently remove OBA-related access controls from existing projects and their subordinate objects. This tool offers a streamlined way to reset or clean up authorization assignments—particularly useful during restructuring, system cleanup, or when transitioning back to standard PFCG role-based authorization. 

For More details on OBA, refer to blog Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) in SA... - SAP Community

OBA functionality provides a more granular and intuitive way to manage user access for specific project objects across selected transactions and use cases. OBA enhances security, improves control, and aligns authorization management with real-world project structures. 

Displaying and Removing Object-Based Authorizations (OBA) from a Project in SAP S/4HANA 

When a project is enabled for Object-Based Authorization (OBA), there may be scenarios where a customer wants to display  the OBA controls to get a complete overview of OBAs assigned within a Project or completely remove OBA controls from a project—for example, to simplify access management or revert to standard role-based authorizations. SAP provides a dedicated report to support this requirement. 

How to Display existing OBA Controls 

To display all OBA-related entries for a given project, execute the transaction CNOBADEL or report CNOBA_DELETE. 

Enter the Project or network and select Display button to display all existing OBA authorizations for the Project/Network. This report will display the OBA for the selected Object and for all the subordinate objects as shown below:- 

The above report can also be adjusted and exported via ALV functions. 

How to Remove OBA Controls 

To delete all OBA-related entries for a given project, execute the transaction CNOBADEL or report CNOBA_DELETE. 

This report clears all object-based authorization records from the relevant tables. After successful execution, the project will no longer be subject to OBA-driven restrictions. 

Using the “Keep Admin OBA” Option During OBA Deletion 

When executing the Deletion transaction/report, SAP provides an additional option—“Keep Admin OBA”—to help customers retain critical administrative access while removing other object-based authorizations. 

If the checkbox Keep Admin OBA is selected before triggering the deletion process, the system will remove all OBA records for every object under the project definition except those with the authorization type Admin (‘3’). 

This means: 

  • All non-admin OBA entries are deleted. 
  • Only OBA records granting administrative access are preserved across all project objects. 

This option is particularly useful when customers want to simplify or reset object-based authorizations while still ensuring that project administrators maintain full access. 

Post-Deletion Access Behavior 

Once OBA entries are completely removed: 

  • The project will rely exclusively on PFCG role-based authorizations for user access control. 
  • In transaction CJ20N, users will be able to view the project data without any OBA limitations. 
  • The Object-Based Authorization tab will no longer appear in the project’s detail screen, confirming that OBA controls have been fully removed. 

If required, we can reactivate OBA via running the report RPSOBA_MIGRATE.

entry.title: Re: Q2 2026: Quarterly Release Highlights for SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
I'm really looking forward to the Technical User feature and glad to see work being done in that area, but I could really do with a Blog on the (planned) features and vision.

The Managing Technical Users is a bit meager and the functionality itself as well. I would for example imagine:

- Being able to manage the users via SCIM
- Call API's to create certificates for them
- Better understand what they're to be used for. Will they replace Application users? All users connecting to BTP? Or even every SAP Cloud product?


Both seem not possible yet.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Week 2 Submission : 

  1. Your srv/game-service.js handler implementations (paste as a JS code block)

    File text :
    (
    const cds = require('@sap/cds')

    module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
    async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting] → CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing → CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing] → CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving → CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
    let newPosition = player.position + roll

    // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
    if (newPosition > 100) {
    await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
    const needed = 100 - player.position
    return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
    }

    // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
    const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
    let finalPosition = newPosition
    let event = null

    if (square?.ladderTo) {
    finalPosition = square.ladderTo
    event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
    finalPosition = square.snakeTo
    event = square.isDoubleHead
    ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    }

    // Persist position and last roll
    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
    .where({ ID: player.ID })

    // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
    if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

    // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
    // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
    // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
    req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting → player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
    // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked → player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
    // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
    req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked] → called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous → CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
    // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished → player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
    await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
    .set({
    finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    winner_ID: player.ID
    })
    .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

    await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    // ── Stretch Goal: skipTurn ────────────────────────────────────────────
    this.on('skipTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ turnsBlocked: (player.turnsBlocked || 0) + 1 })
    .where({ ID: player.ID })
    })

    // ── CHEAT CODE: Teleport player for testing ───────────────────────────
    this.on('cheatPosition', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const targetSquare = req.data.pos
    const playerID = req.params[0].ID

    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ position: targetSquare })
    .where({ ID: playerID })

    return req.reply(`Player teleported to square ${targetSquare}`)
    })

    return super.init()
    }
    }

    ) 
    Added a cheat Code to change the position of the player and USIING  POST you can modify the Players position to desirable positions
  2. A screenshot showing the HTTP 409 response when calling rollDice in the wrong state

     

  3. A screenshot or OData response showing your player's position after landing on a snake or ladder

     

  4. One sentence: what happens if you call winGame on a player whose position is not 100? How does your implementation handle it?
    If you call winGame prematurely, the implementation will still successfully mark the game session as finished and declare that player the winner because there is no explicit validation check in the handler verifying that their position is exactly 100.

entry.title: Re: PR Email Notification Issues Incorrect Deadline Date/...
entry.content:
For PR rejection emails, SAP KBA 3470474 confirms that the Decision Note is not included in the standard CDS views by current system design; only the Decision Reason Text can be maintained in SPRO. 

For the deadline issue, please provide the S/4HANA release and deployment model before recommending.

entry.title: Re: SAF-T Portugal - Incorrect Opening Balance calculatio...
entry.content: Based on the analysis of the accounting documents and balances, the SAF-T behavior appears to be working as designed. The SAF-T Opening Balance is being derived from the balance carryforward (the closing balance of the previous fiscal year). Since account 4310000001 had a balance of EUR 56,863.33 on 31/12/2024, this is the value used by the SAF-T as the opening balance. However, during the S/4HANA migration, an opening balance of EUR 113,726.66 (double the expected amount) was loaded into the account. The journal entries posted in January and March were then created assuming this duplicated opening balance. Later, additional adjustment postings were made so that the account ended with the correct balance. This creates the inconsistency observed today: The General Ledger and trial balance are mathematically correct because all subsequent postings were made based on an opening balance of EUR 113,726.66. The SAF-T starts from EUR 56,863.33, as it reads the balance carryforward from the previous fiscal year. When the January and March movements are applied to this opening balance, the resulting balance no longer reconciles. Therefore, the issue does not appear to be an error in the SAF-T calculation itself. Rather, there are two different opening balance references: the opening balance created during the migration process and subsequently used for accounting postings; and the balance carryforward used by the SAF-T report. The main challenge is that correcting the issue by posting additional journal entries is unlikely to solve it. Any new adjustment would modify the General Ledger balances and could also affect the final G/L accounts to which the migration balances have already been transferred. In other words, it is possible to correct the SAF-T while creating inconsistencies in the accounting records, or vice versa. For this reason, although correcting the issue "at the source" is theoretically the right approach, it is not clear how this should be done in a productive system where the migration has already been completed, accounting periods have been closed, and subsequent postings depend on the current balances. Given the complexity of the situation and the potential accounting impact, we believe the best approach is to open an SAP incident under FI-LOC-SAF-PT / Document and Reporting Compliance. SAP should be able to confirm: whether this behavior is expected in this migration scenario; whether there is a supported procedure to correct the migrated opening balance; or whether a different correction approach is recommended for productive systems. Considering the potential impact on the accounting history, we recommend obtaining SAP's official guidance before making any changes to the productive system.

entry.title: Re: "No personal master record exists for person 0"
entry.content:
Greetings,

you neet to have properly maintained business partners. The following data must be integrated / synchronized:

  • business partner data
  • HR number 
  • SAP username

Based on this combination system knows to which BP the operation is being assigned when you click "Assign to me".

See SAP NOTE: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003682426 

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: MIGO Transfer Posting Against Reservation
entry.content:
In MIGO, when you perform a Transfer Posting against a Reservation, the system behavior is to display all reservation line items, regardless of whether they are already issued or still pending. This is standard SAP design because:

  • Reservation line items are not dynamically reduced from the display once issued. Even if the material has been fully withdrawn, the line remains visible in MIGO.
  • The system differentiates between issued vs. pending quantities using the “Removed Quantity” and “Requirement Quantity” fields in the reservation item.

This means the user must carefully check “Removed Quantity” and “Requirement Quantity” to avoid attempting postings against items that are already fully consumed. If your business process requires hiding fully issued items, you would need to consider a custom filter or enhancement in MIGO (e.g., via BAdI MB_MIGO_BADI) to suppress fully issued lines, or using transaction MB1B with movement type directly against the reservation, where the system prompts only for open quantities.
